memoryArtifacts.headline: 1629 Burial Joane Hull In Crewkerne
memoryArtifacts.description: Sept [Likely Latin for Sepultus est, meaning buried]   Joane the wife of Thomas Hull was buried the same day [as the above entry, which was 30 October].

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Pysing於1551年出生在英國薩默塞特可福可納時，她的父親Richard Alexander Pysing30歲，母親Margery Ann Evered23歲。她於1572年1月11日與Thomas Hull the Younger在英國薩默塞特可福可納結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1629年10月30日在她的家鄉享年78歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特可福可納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Pysing于1551年出生在英国薩默塞特可福可納时，她的父亲Richard Alexander Pysing30岁，母亲Margery Ann Evered23岁。她于1572年1月11日与Thomas Hull the Younger在英国薩默塞特可福可納结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1629年10月30日在她的家乡享年78岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特可福可納。

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Pysing was born in 1551, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England, her father, Richard Alexander Pysing, was 30 and her mother, Margery Ann Evered, was 23. She married Thomas Hull the Younger on 11 January 1572, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 30 October 1629, in her hometown, at the age of 78, and was buried in Crewkerne, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Pysing est née en 1551, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Richard Alexander Pysing, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margery Ann Evered, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Hull the Younger le 11 janvier 1572, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 octobre 1629, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterrée à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Pysing 1551 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Alexander Pysing 30 und ihre Mutter Margery Ann Evered 23. Sie hat am 11. Januar 1572 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England Thomas Hull the Younger geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. Oktober 1629 im Alter von 78 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Crewkerne, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Pysing è nata il nel 1551 nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England, sua padre, Richard Alexander Pysing, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Margery Ann Evered, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Hull the Younger il 11 gennaio 1572, nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 30 ottobre 1629, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolta a Crewkerne, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Pysingが1551年にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンで生まれた時，父Richard Alexander Pysingは30歳，母Margery Ann Everedは23歳であった。1572年1月11日にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンでThomas Hull the Youngerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1629年10月30日に故郷で78歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトクレウケルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Pysing(이)가 1551년 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Alexander Pysing(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Ann Evered(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1572년 1월 11일 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 Thomas Hull the Younger(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1629년 10월 30일 그녀의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Pysing nasceu em 1551, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Alexander Pysing, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Margery Ann Evered, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Hull the Younger em 11 de janeiro de 1572, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de outubro de 1629, em sua cidade natal, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Pysing родилась в 1551 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Alexander Pysing было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Ann Evered, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Hull the Younger 11 января 1572 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 30 октября 1629 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Pysing nació en 1551, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Alexander Pysing, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Margery Ann Evered, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Thomas Hull the Younger el 11 de enero de 1572, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 30 de octubre de 1629, en su pueblo, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultada en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra.
